reasoning: Bobby Portis was suspended for 25 games by the NBA on February 20, 2025 (reportedly related to a drug suspension). Because of this prolonged absence during the crucial final stretch of the regular season and the negative optics associated with a suspension, his chances of winning the Sixth Man of the Year award are practically zero. Furthermore, Payton Pritchard was already established as the heavy odds-on favorite (-175) well before Portis's suspension, and the Polymarket odds for Portis were dead at 0.00 in early March. There is no plausible scenario where Portis wins this award, thus the minimal probability of 2% is highly appropriate.
